text: The surgeon carefully dissects and removes adhesions to expose the inferior edge of the right lobe of the liver. The hepatic flexure of the colon is moved aside to facilitate safe lateral to medial dissection, aiming to identify the gallbladder at the fundus or lateral aspect of the body.

The surgeon considers using intraoperative ultrasound to assist in identifying the gallbladder. If ultrasound is unavailable or ineffective, a cholangiogram is performed through the cholecystostomy tube. The cholecystostomy tube is dissected free to its entry point into the gallbladder.

The surgeon performs a top-down cholecystectomy, carefully dissecting directly on the gallbladder wall to avoid the liver parenchyma. The body of the gallbladder is visibly freed from the liver fossa, with the cholecystostomy tube still in place.

The surgeon uses adequate liver retraction to expose the cystic structures. Additional ports are considered for better access. In challenging cases, the gallbladder is transected, allowing interior visualization of the infundibulum to determine the necessary dissection extent for isolating the cystic duct without leaving remnant gallbladder tissue.

The surgeon transects the gallbladder at the neck, revealing an impacted stone inside. The stone is removed and placed aside for later retrieval. After cleaning out residual debris, the interior view shows the infundibulum narrowing into the cystic duct, with surrounding tissue being dissected off the cystic duct.

The surgeon applies lateral retraction and performs careful dissection around the gallbladder to avoid injury to nearby portal structures. During the top-down cholecystectomy, a small defect is created in the cystic duct as the surrounding tissue is cleared, while the cystic artery is divided at its terminal branches.

The surgeon secures the cystic duct with an endo-loop, ensuring sufficient length for safe closure. Depending on the duct size, clips, loops, stapling devices, or intracorporeal sutures may be used. After securing the duct, the remaining gallbladder is resected, and all gallbladder pieces and stones are collected.
